---
name: MIP stub
about: Socialize a change before writing the full MIP. Looking for co-authors, prior art, or a sanity check.
title: "[mip-stub] <short description>"
labels: ["mip-stub"]
---

## One-paragraph abstract

_What would the MIP do? Plain language — the technical detail belongs in the MIP itself._

## Problem

_What does this solve that the spec can't today? Cite the failure, gap, or pain — not "wouldn't it be nice if…"_

## Sketch of proposal

_Enough design to evaluate the idea. Wire-format changes, new block types, new DNS records, modified bridge rules, etc. Rough is fine._

## Alternatives you have considered

_At least one. "No alternatives" usually means the design isn't considered enough yet._

## Who would implement this

_Name at least one implementer — yourself, a named provider, a client maintainer. Core MIPs require two independent implementers by Final per [MIP-0001](../../mips/mip-0001-process.md)._

## What category fits

- [ ] Core
- [ ] Content
- [ ] Bridge
- [ ] Interface
- [ ] Process
- [ ] Informational

## Prior art

_Existing RFCs, MIPs, other protocols, academic work. Linking prior art and explaining how your design differs is the minimum bar._

## Open questions for the stub stage

_What do you want early reviewers to weigh in on before you invest in the full MIP?_

---

_When you're ready to draft the full MIP, copy [`mips/mip-template.md`](../../mips/mip-template.md) to `mips/mip-XXXX-short-title.md` and open a PR. Link this stub from the PR description._
